jokes.text: The formal complaint letter signed 'Disgruntled' being read aloud with its petty grievances
jokes.setup: A serious-sounding complaint letter about club management
jokes.punchline: The complaints are hilariously petty - packaged turkey roll, stale bread, tucked shirts
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central mystery with genuinely funny petty complaints that feel authentic to Larry's world

jokes.text: Who is Disgruntled?
jokes.setup: Takahashi's angry reaction to the complaint letter
jokes.punchline: The dramatic, almost noir-like delivery of this simple question
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for the episode's central mystery with Takahashi's intensity

jokes.text: I am not Disgruntled. I mean, I'm disgruntled, but I'm not Disgruntled.
jokes.setup: Larry being accused of writing the complaint
jokes.punchline: The distinction between being generally disgruntled vs. being 'Disgruntled' the anonymous complainer
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David wordplay that perfectly captures his character - pedantic distinction between states of being disgruntled

jokes.text: The breakfast menu cutoff at exactly 11:00 AM
jokes.setup: They arrive at 11:10 and can't order breakfast
jokes.punchline: The arbitrary nature of the 10-minute difference and Larry's incredulity
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David observation about arbitrary rules, relatable frustration

jokes.text: It's like a Broadway play where they have to put in a new set. And so now they're putting in the lunch set.
jokes.setup: Trying to understand why breakfast can't be made after 11
jokes.punchline: Comparing a restaurant kitchen to elaborate Broadway staging
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd analogy that takes the restaurant's logic to its extreme conclusion

jokes.text: Larry's 'breakfast loophole' - deconstructing Cobb salad for breakfast components
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get around breakfast cutoff
jokes.punchline: Server knows this scheme and calls it 'breakfast loophole'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - trying to game the system with elaborate workarounds

jokes.text: You're looking for a breakfast loophole. It's not gonna work. And you're not the first person to try.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get eggs from the Cobb salad
jokes.punchline: The waitress has dealt with this exact scheme before
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Larry isn't as clever as he thinks, waitress has seen it all

jokes.text: It was Five Easy Pieces, remember? With Jack Nicholson.
jokes.setup: Larry's attempt at ordering food creatively
jokes.punchline: Reference to famous movie scene about working around menu restrictions
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference, doesn't add much beyond the obvious parallel

jokes.text: Larry bringing his own organic eggs to the restaurant
jokes.setup: Larry's particular food preferences
jokes.punchline: The absurdity and rudeness of bringing your own ingredients
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David behavior - obsessive about details to the point of social awkwardness

jokes.text: And then it gives men breasts. You know, Larry could grow breasts.
jokes.setup: Discussion about antibiotics in eggs
jokes.punchline: The visual image of Larry developing breasts from bad eggs
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing visual but fairly predictable gross-out humor

jokes.text: I'd be pretty cute. Pretty, pretty, pretty cute.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry potentially growing breasts
jokes.punchline: Larry's vanity and his signature 'pretty, pretty, pretty' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Nice use of Larry's catchphrase in an unexpected context, shows his narcissism

jokes.text: No, it's a horrible idea. It's a terrible idea... Because it's a wa... It's a... It doesn't do anything. It's a waste of time.
jokes.setup: Suggestion of couples therapy
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate, stammering, emphatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry reaction - immediate dismissal of anything therapeutic or touchy-feely

jokes.text: Larry's violent objection to couples therapy contrasted with Irma's recovery needs
jokes.setup: Irma desperately needs therapy for recovery
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate hostile rejection despite her vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry selfishness but the setup feels a bit manipulative

jokes.text: The breakfast chaos after Larry breaks the 11 AM rule
jokes.setup: Larry gets breakfast after 11 AM
jokes.punchline: Multiple customers demanding breakfast food, creating chaos
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Larry's rule-breaking creates chaos for everyone

jokes.text: What the fuck? Much healthier. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Jeff discovers his wife changed his order while he was in bathroom
jokes.punchline: His outrage followed by Susie's matter-of-fact justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the Greenes' dysfunctional dynamic

jokes.text: I'm Disgruntled. It's me. I'm Disgruntled.
jokes.setup: The mystery of who wrote the complaint letter
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confession with almost pride in his anonymous complaint
jokes.explanation: Major revelation delivered perfectly, Jeff's pride in his petty complaints is very funny

jokes.text: You tacked that thing up like you were Martin Luther.
jokes.setup: Jeff's confession about posting the complaint letter
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jeff's petty club complaints to Martin Luther's 95 Theses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical comparison that elevates petty complaints to world-changing protest

jokes.text: It's the best thing you've ever done in your life. Have I done any other good things? No. So this is the only one? This is the only decent thing. I've ever done? You've ever done.
jokes.setup: Larry praising Jeff's complaint letter
jokes.punchline: The conversation revealing Jeff has literally never done anything good before
jokes.explanation: Great escalating insult disguised as praise, perfectly captures Larry's backhanded compliments

jokes.text: That should be on your tombstone, 'I'm Disgruntled.'
jokes.setup: Jeff's pride in being Disgruntled
jokes.punchline: Suggesting it as his epitaph
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny suggestion that Jeff's petty complaints are his greatest legacy

jokes.text: Liberal darling. Get the hell out of here.
jokes.setup: Willie calling Larry a liberal darling
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive reaction to being called progressive
jokes.explanation: Larry's discomfort with political labels, stays true to character

jokes.text: Who? Who called me an asshole? ... Although one person did call you an asshole.
jokes.setup: Willie casually mentioning someone called Larry an asshole
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate obsession with finding out who
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David obsession - can't let go of knowing who insulted him

jokes.text: But it's a universal fact that everybody is called an asshole! I'm sure Mother Teresa was called an asshole by someone.
jokes.setup: Larry's defense against being called an asshole
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim that even Mother Teresa was called an asshole
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic that takes Larry's defensiveness to an absurd extreme with Mother Teresa

jokes.text: Oh, look at Miss Perfect helping the lepers.
jokes.setup: Continuing the Mother Teresa bit
jokes.punchline: Larry's imagined sarcastic criticism of Mother Teresa's charity work
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up showing Larry's cynical worldview - even saints have haters

jokes.text: It was Troy. The Deaf actor at the club... I hit him with a golf ball because I couldn't yell 'fore.'
jokes.setup: Larry guessing who called him an asshole
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous chain of events involving a deaf Oscar winner
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Larry David scenario - disability comedy done with perfect awkwardness

jokes.text: What, do you think he signed to me that you're an asshole?
jokes.setup: Discussion of the deaf actor Troy calling Larry an asshole
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation of 'signing' that you're an asshole
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'signing' that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: I am most certainly not Disgruntled with a capital D. I'm disgruntled with a small D, Willie.
jokes.setup: Willie asking if Larry is Disgruntled
jokes.punchline: Larry's precise distinction between big D and little D disgruntled
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with Larry's pedantic precision about capitalization

jokes.text: The J.G. Wentworth commercial singing between Larry and Irma
jokes.setup: Waiting for therapy session
jokes.punchline: Getting caught up in an annoying commercial jingle together
jokes.explanation: Relatable moment about catchy jingles, but not particularly original or clever

jokes.text: I didn't know I was seeing you. Oh! I thought I was seeing Melanie Stainback.
jokes.setup: Larry arrives at therapy
jokes.punchline: Discovering his therapist is someone he knows personally
jokes.explanation: Good setup for awkward therapy session, creates natural tension

jokes.text: He has a thing about phallic-shaped vegetables... I see him just stroking, stroking an eggplant.
jokes.setup: Irma sharing personal details in therapy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre sexual accusation about Larry and vegetables
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable oversharing that creates cringe comedy, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Irma's graphic description of Larry stroking phallic vegetables
jokes.setup: Therapy session supposed to be safe space
jokes.punchline: Irma shares embarrassing intimate details about Larry
jokes.explanation: Relies on crude humor without much sophistication

jokes.text: Yeah, I'd eat a cucumber if it was square. I don't care that it's phallic shaped.
jokes.setup: Irma's accusation about phallic vegetables
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical defense about vegetable shapes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry defense - practical and logical while completely missing the point

jokes.text: Does your husband have a very low-hanging scrotal sac? A lot of guys do.
jokes.setup: Therapist trying to redirect from Larry's complaints
jokes.punchline: Her immediate pivot to extremely personal anatomy questions
jokes.explanation: Awkward therapy overshare that escalates the discomfort. Gets reaction but somewhat cheap shock humor.

jokes.text: Does your husband have a very low-hanging scrotal sac? ... When he goes to the bathroom in the night... I hear them slapping on his thighs.
jokes.setup: Therapy session getting too personal
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of Hobie's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that creates discomfort, relies more on shock than cleverness

jokes.text: It sounds like, you know... A flip-flop in a Mexican resort.
jokes.setup: Description of testicle sounds
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd comparison to vacation sounds
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific and vivid comparison that elevates the crude humor

jokes.text: He's never fucking home... But the issue is, he hates the fucking place.
jokes.setup: Susie complaining about Jeff in therapy
jokes.punchline: The contradiction that he's never home but she's happy about it
jokes.explanation: Good insight into the Greenes' marriage dynamic, typical Susie honesty

jokes.text: What's going on over there? You guys hear the sneeze?
jokes.setup: Sensitive therapy discussion
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoia about being overheard
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the privacy breach plot, shows Larry's hypervigilance

jokes.text: He's the guy in the next office! He's best friends with Takahashi. He heard everything we said.
jokes.setup: Larry recognizing the doctor next door
jokes.punchline: Realizing his secret is compromised
jokes.explanation: Good plot escalation that creates new complications from the therapy session

jokes.text: I got one question. What's a urologist? ... It's nothing. You pee in a cup.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Leon to spy on the therapy session
jokes.punchline: Leon's innocent question about what he's signing up for
jokes.explanation: Sets up Leon's involvement with his characteristic naivety

jokes.text: I love peeing in cups. You know what I'm saying? I like peeing in big-ass bowls, but I'll pee in a cup.
jokes.setup: Leon agreeing to go to the urologist
jokes.punchline: Leon's enthusiasm for urinating in various containers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon character moment - turning mundane medical procedure into personal preference

jokes.text: The man that makes corn chips on the cob is gonna be a fucking billionaire.
jokes.setup: Random Leon observation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre business idea about corn chips
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random Leon observation that's both absurd and somehow logical

jokes.text: I walked into the council chamber naked, and all I remember is the air on my nipples and the looks of admiration and horror.
jokes.setup: Irma describing her rock bottom moment
jokes.punchline: Vivid, uncomfortable details of public nudity
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable oversharing that creates cringe comedy, specific details make it memorable

jokes.text: That's why they call him Long Ball Larry. Just kidding, Larry. That's a good one. That's gonna stick. Long Ball Larry.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry's golf game
jokes.punchline: Nickname that references the earlier testicle conversation
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that connects golf to the therapy session, Larry's acceptance of it is funny

jokes.text: I should've gotten the eggs. I was rushed into pancakes. You rushed me into pancakes. You did.
jokes.setup: Melanie complaining about her food choice
jokes.punchline: Blame escalation over a simple food order
jokes.explanation: Minor escalation that shows how small disputes grow, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I should've gotten the eggs. I was rushed into pancakes.
jokes.setup: Irma unhappy with her breakfast choice
jokes.punchline: Blaming Larry for rushing her into the wrong decision
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier scene but not particularly strong on its own.

jokes.text: Something's amiss with the eggs... I know that you were a little upset the other day when I got breakfast after 11:00. Maybe you got a little breakfast grudge?
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about his eggs
jokes.punchline: Accusing the waitress of having a 'breakfast grudge'
jokes.explanation: Great phrase 'breakfast grudge' that captures Larry's paranoid worldview perfectly

jokes.text: Are you Disgruntled? No, I am not Disgruntled... Yeah, that's you. Yeah. I do scowl. And I will admit to muttering.
jokes.setup: Takahashi accusing Larry of being Disgruntled
jokes.punchline: Larry's admission to scowling and muttering while denying the larger charge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Larry admits to his general demeanor while maintaining technical innocence

jokes.text: This is 21 dollars at Supercuts.
jokes.setup: Larry insulting Willie's expensive haircut
jokes.punchline: Willie's matter-of-fact correction about his cheap haircut
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that deflates Larry's class-based insult

jokes.text: This one's caramel colored, and this one has an almond sheen. A sheen? I don't see a sheen on this. There's a sheen. Yeah. Eggs don't even have a sheen. Eggs have a sheen. No sheen here. There's a sheen, Willie!
jokes.setup: Larry examining eggs in the kitchen
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate insistence about egg sheen that no one else can see
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David obsessiveness - finding distinctions no one else can see and becoming increasingly manic about it

jokes.text: Sometimes I pee right in the condom, and I'll just take it off, tie a little knot in that motherfucker and throw it in the trash, and keep on fucking.
jokes.setup: Leon at the urologist talking about urination habits
jokes.punchline: Graphic TMI about his sex life to a medical professional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon character moment - inappropriate oversharing in medical setting

jokes.text: You ain't no piss doctor! You a ass doctor! ... You don't do that shit to people, man!
jokes.setup: Leon getting a prostate exam unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: His outraged reaction to the surprise nature of the exam
jokes.explanation: Leon's colorful terminology and outrage at medical procedures creates great physical comedy

jokes.text: My whole life, I hated strawberries... And then one day, I had a fresh strawberry right from the ground... Changed my life... It's like people, Willie. You don't really know them until you taste them.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to make a philosophical point about judging
jokes.punchline: The creepy implication of 'tasting' people to know them
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David philosophical rambling that takes an unexpected and disturbing turn

jokes.text: There's a lot of fruit out there, and I think we're bumping up against our time, and I kind of see us going all the way down the produce aisle, so I'm gonna stop you there.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing his fruit analogies
jokes.punchline: Willie's professional but exasperated attempt to end the tangent
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about Larry's tendency to go off on tangents, Willie as straight man

jokes.text: You are in big trouble, because you are Disgruntled! I'm Disgruntled! What, you? Yes. I'm Disgruntled. I'm Disgruntled. I'm Disgruntled. I'm Disgruntled, too. I'm Disgruntled. Baka!
jokes.setup: The revelation and confrontation about Disgruntled
jokes.punchline: Everyone claiming to be Disgruntled in a chaotic scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and payoff to the episode's central mystery, absurdist chaos as everyone claims the identity

jokes.text: The chef at the club is eating all of Larry's organic fucking eggs? I knew it!
jokes.setup: Learning about the club situation
jokes.punchline: Confirmation of Larry's paranoid theory about his eggs
jokes.explanation: Good payoff confirming Larry was right to be paranoid about his eggs being switched
